notes: Lab-measured drop 9.6mm vs brand-claimed 5mm. Energy return 65.4%, midsole softness 39.5 AC. Neutral daily trainer with above-average stability (torsional rigidity 4/5, heel counter 4/5). Narrow tapered toebox. Knit upper with perfect 5/5 breathability. Brand weight 235g, lab 232g.

Lab-measured values used (heel 40.9mm, forefoot 33.5mm, drop 7.4mm, weight 254g). ASICS claims 41.5mm stack and 8mm drop. Softest Novablast yet (30.3 AC). AHAR LO outsole, jacquard mesh upper, late mild forefoot rocker. Despite stack >=40mm it's lightweight, classified as daily_trainer not max_cushion.

Lab-measured drop 11.4mm vs Nike claimed 10mm; lab heel 33.6mm/forefoot 22.2mm vs brand 37/27mm. Features dual Air Zoom units (heel and forefoot), rockered sole. Low traction (0.25). True to size. $10 price increase from v40.

Nylon winged plate. Pebax-based PWRRUN PB foam, firmer than v3 (38.9 AC). Energy return 72.0% heel / 73.4% forefoot. Tempo super-trainer, neutral. Slightly small fit, narrow tapered toebox. Wet traction 0.50. Brand weight/drop differ: 235g, 8.0mm drop.

Neutral daily trainer with mild stability features (reinforced sidewalls, stiff 5/5 heel counter). Lab-measured values used (drop 12.4mm lab vs 12.0mm brand; heel 35.1mm, forefoot 22.7mm). Midsole firmness 41.5 AC (firmer than average). Breathability 4/5. Segmented Crash Pad outsole. Forefoot stack also cited as 22.8mm in text.

Lab-measured values used (brand claims 32/27mm, 5mm drop). Firm EVA-based midsole (40.5 AC), low energy return (53%). Narrow fit; below-average wet grip (0.33). Very stiff heel counter (5/5). Mild unobtrusive stability suitable for mild overpronation but no medial post.

Daily max-cushion trainer with PureGEL heel insert. Brand drop claimed 8mm, lab measured 8.4mm. Energy return low (48.3%). Exceptionally torsionally rigid (5/5) despite no plate. Outsole mixes AHAR+ and ASICSGRIP rubber; outstanding wet traction 0.85. Stiff sole gives surprising stability though not a dedicated stability shoe.
Brand-claimed drop is 8mm; lab measured 8.5mm. Outsole thickness 3.4mm. Neutral daily trainer with wide base providing stability. Subpar breathability (2/5). Tapered toebox. Excellent durability.

PEBA-based Dreamstrike+ foam with 71.2% energy return; uses EVA-carrier 'Support Rods' (not a carbon/nylon plate) for stability and durability. Neutral shoe but tests as very stable due to firm secondary foam, heel bevel, and stiff heel counter. Dropped Continental rubber for Adiwear. Lab drop 9.7mm (brand 10mm). Fits slightly small with wide, high-volume toebox.

Plate is a Pebax-based Wave Plate that runs only to the midfoot (not full-length), so plate_material set to 'none' per allowed enum (carbon/nylon/none); actual material is Pebax. Energy return low (49.9%). Heel-focused daily trainer. Outsole rubber 3.8mm thick, very durable.

Dual-foam midsole: supercritical Nitro foam top, firmer EVA carrier foam below. Narrow forefoot/toebox (70.4mm toebox, 107.7mm forefoot midsole). PumaGrip outsole, traction 0.60. Energy return 56.2%, shock absorption 127 SA. Neutral trainer with above-average torsional rigidity. Brand-spec drop listed as 10mm; lab measured 9.3mm.

Lab-measured drop 6.2mm vs brand-claimed 4mm. Stack measured by caliper; brand claims 39mm heel/35mm forefoot. Outsole thickness 3mm, exposed foam. Narrow midfoot and toebox (92.2mm width, 69.2mm toebox). Low energy return (51%). Neutral arch, rocker geometry. Heavy at 311g. Archetype max_cushion due to large platform and heavy weight, though heel stack just under 38mm.

Neutral max-cushioned daily trainer with knit upper; runs warm (breathability 3/5). Rigid 5/5 heel counter favors heel strikers. Flexible (torsional rigidity 2/5). Roadtack Rubber outsole, coefficient of friction 0.48. GTS 21 version has GuideRails for stability. Brand weight 277g; lab weight 278g. Lab drop 10.6mm vs brand 10.0mm.

Maximalist neutral daily trainer; lab heel stack 38mm (ASICS states 41.5mm), forefoot 30.2mm (stated 33.5mm), drop 7.8mm (stated 8mm). Soft midsole (18.6 HA), wide base feels stable despite high stack. Narrow toebox, poor breathability. RunRepeat score 81. Weight is men's size 9.

Lab-measured drop 9.7mm; brand advertises 10mm drop. Neutral daily trainer with full-length PWRRUN PB midsole. Energy return 60.9%. Some non-intrusive stability features (sidewalls, broad midfoot) but not a stability shoe. Firm heel counter (4/5). Breathability rated 3/5. Traction 0.43. Heavy maximalist trainer with stack >=38mm placed in max_cushion.

Maximalist stability shoe with 4D Guidance System, midsole sidewalls, rigid heel counter, and PureGEL in heel. Drop measured 11.5mm in lab vs 10mm advertised. Energy return low (49.2%). Excellent traction (0.83). Breathability 4/5. Ideal for heel strikers; not for forefoot strikers. Although marketed as stability, RunRepeat notes it lacks a traditional medial post, using non-intrusive support features instead.

Uses GuideRails support technology (no traditional medial post). Lab-measured drop 12.6mm vs brand-stated 12mm. Brand weight 10.4oz/294g. Forefoot stack thin; midsole softness 19.5 HA. Outsole thickness 3.6mm. Poor toebox durability (1/5). Wet forefoot traction 0.47.
Moderate stability shoe using midsole walls, sole flares, and rocker instead of medial post. Lab drop 7mm vs brand 6mm. PWRRUN+ sockliner. Wet traction score 0.44. Midsole softness 22.3 HA.

Mild stability via Hoka J-Frame technology (firmer foam, no traditional medial post). Brand-stated drop 5mm; lab measured 6.3mm. Firm CMEVA midsole (26.6 HA), low energy return (52.2% heel/56.1% forefoot). Narrow tapered toebox. Stiff (5/5 torsional rigidity, 5/5 heel counter). Wet traction 0.40. Breathability 3/5.

Uses full-length fiberglass EnergyRods (not carbon plate, hence plate_material=none despite has_plate=true). Lab weight 261g. Continental rubber outsole (wet-grip 0.57). Lightstrike 2.0 softness 38.3 AC, Lightstrike Pro 29.2 AC. Energy return 63.5% heel. Best for midfoot/forefoot strikers; poor toebox durability (1/5); flexible heel counter (1/5). Brand drop 6.5mm, brand stack heel 37/forefoot 30.5mm. Rocker geometry present.

Lab-measured drop 9.6mm vs brand-stated 8mm; brand weight 211g for v3, v4 lab weight 237g. Carbon plate slightly adjusted for training. FF Turbo only a small forefoot puck despite full-length marketing. Heel stack 42.5mm exceeds World Athletics 40mm race limit. Low energy return (55.5% heel, 61.8% forefoot), narrow fit (93.6mm), poor stability for neutral runners only. Marketed as a super trainer. ASICSGRIP outsole, not for gravel.

Dual-foam midsole (Nitro bottom, Nitro Elite top). Lab drop 10.1mm vs brand 10mm. Narrow fit and toebox (91.5mm width, 69.7mm toebox). PumaGrip outsole with strong wet/dry traction (0.65). Subtle rocker, knit upper with PWRTAPE. Not recommended for stability needs. Brand weight listed 9.5oz/269g; lab 268g.

Marathon super shoe with Nike Air Pods in forefoot and carbon Flyplate. Lab weight 201g; brand weight 200g. Official drop 8mm, lab measured 8.5mm. Dual-density ZoomX (lower 34.5 AC, firmer upper 47.2 AC). Outsole durability rated bad; better suited for midfoot/forefoot strikers.

Lab-measured values reported: heel 37.8mm, forefoot 29.8mm (brand stated 33mm), drop 8mm (brand stated 6.5mm), weight 218g (brand 223g). Uses carbon-infused Energyrods rather than a traditional plate. Continental rubber outsole; very stable racer for midfoot/forefoot strikers; not suited for heel strikers. Has medial cutout. RunRepeat score 92.

Dual-midsole: PWRRUN HG top (33.5 AC) over PWRRUN PB bottom (35.1 AC) with S-shaped carbon plate. Lab heel drop 9.5mm vs brand-listed 8mm. Energy return 71.7%, wet traction 0.52. Stiffness 25.7N. Heavier than v3. Stack >=35mm and weight <240g with carbon plate qualifies as carbon_racer.

Marathon racer. Lab heel stack 33.5mm, forefoot 30.8mm, lab drop 2.7mm (brand lists 39/34mm, 5mm drop). Lab weight 205g (US men's 9). Carbon plate moved closer to foot makes forefoot stiffer (25.3N). Outsole rubber thin at 2.0mm with poor durability. Energy return 70.1% heel / 74.4% forefoot. Wet traction 0.84. Narrow fit (92.2mm). RunRepeat review notes considers stack max-cushioned but weight is low; classified carbon_racer per plate+weight criteria.

Lab drop 6.6mm vs Hoka's stated 5mm. Fits half size small with narrow toebox. Energy return 74.2%, qualifies as super shoe. Stiff (80.7N), very durable outsole. Brand weight listed as 224g; lab weight 218g.

Vibram outsole; neutral arch support. Lab-measured weight 277g (US M9), brand claims 276g; women's 241g. Lab drop 3.8mm vs brand 4mm. Stack includes 2.8mm insole. Very soft midsole, low energy return. Narrow fit, not for wide feet. Price listed as $155 in comparison table, $320 elsewhere (ambiguous).

Lab-measured drop 14.1mm (brand 10mm); feels like 8-10mm. No rock plate but thick Contragrip rubber outsole. Very firm midsole (52.9 AC), low energy return (47.4%), poor breathability (1/5). Optimized for muddy/winter terrain and heel strikers.

Rock plate in forefoot (material not specified). Drop measured 9.2mm in lab vs 8mm brand claim. TrailTack Green outsole, Trail Adapt system. Heavier than v16. Firmer DNA Loft v2 (25.5 HA) tuned for stability. Wide toebox. Heel/toebox durability poor.

Rock plate (not carbon/nylon, so plate_material=none). Lab-measured drop 2.2mm vs brand 4.0mm; stack values are lab-measured. PWRRUN+ plush insole. PWRTRAC outsole. 2E width available in select markets. Not for ultras; somewhat firm ride. RunRepeat score 83.
